emails.subject: Willets Point install coordination
emails.preview: Following up on the March invoice and delivery schedule
emails.summary: Saemi is asking about the Willets Point delivery timeline. She needs confirmation on the install date and wants to know if the March invoice ($65,158) has been processed. She also mentioned coordinating access to the building for the install crew.

replyStrategies.label: Confirm timeline
replyStrategies.description: Confirm the install date and let her know the invoice was sent, ask about building access requirements

replyStrategies.label: Request more info
replyStrategies.description: Ask Saemi for the preferred install window and any site requirements before confirming

replyStrategies.label: Delay tactfully
replyStrategies.description: Let her know we're finalizing the schedule and will confirm within 48 hours

emails.subject: Re: Art - PRC
emails.preview: Pending response on invoice sent March 29
emails.summary: You sent Bill an invoice on March 29 for artwork selected to date at the PRC project, along with a budget outlook. He hasn't responded in 11 days. The project has a mid-June installation deadline and payment is needed to procure artwork and commissions.

replyStrategies.label: Gentle follow-up
replyStrategies.description: Politely check on payment timing, emphasize the mid-June deadline

replyStrategies.label: Escalate urgency
replyStrategies.description: Stress that procurement timelines are at risk without payment, request expedited processing

replyStrategies.label: Offer to discuss
replyStrategies.description: Suggest a quick call to walk through the invoice and budget if there are any questions

emails.subject: Office Art Program - Estimates
emails.summary: You sent Peter estimates for all 5 Ascot office locations (Ridgeland, Dallas, Denver, Phoenix, NYC) on April 6. You mentioned the NYC frames would be delivered Friday and other offices need 2-4 weeks for fabrication. Waiting on his approval to send a 50% deposit invoice.

replyStrategies.label: Check in
replyStrategies.description: Quick follow-up asking if he's had a chance to review the estimates

replyStrategies.label: Provide update
replyStrategies.description: Share a progress update on the NYC delivery and ask if estimates look good for the other offices

replyStrategies.label: Hold off
replyStrategies.description: It's only been 3 days - give him more time before following up

emails.subject: 2025 Tax Returns
emails.preview: Balance due $114,805 IRS + $43,057 NY by Apr 15
emails.summary: Dylan sent the 2025 tax summary: you owe $114,805 to IRS and $43,057 to NY (includes $5,068 underpayment interest). Q1 2026 estimated taxes of $32,000 IRS + $12,170 NY are also due April 15. Returns are with the tax director for review. Dylan asked about Calvine's childcare expenses ($5,000 dependent care benefits).

replyStrategies.label: Confirm and pay
replyStrategies.description: Confirm the numbers look right, ask for payment instructions, confirm childcare was used

replyStrategies.label: Ask questions
replyStrategies.description: Ask about reducing the underpayment penalty or setting up estimated payments going forward

replyStrategies.label: Request extension
replyStrategies.description: Ask if filing an extension is advisable while the returns are still in review
